fields.description:
## Context
A work ticket carries a single attestation slot (`state["signature"]`), written
last-writer-wins by `process_signature` (`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`). The
plan-review attestation (claim time) and the completion verdict (close time) share that
slot, so closing destroys the plan-review attestation (bug grumpy-site-beard /
e6a0-ba9b-ae92-43d7). The two are independent proofs and must coexist; we also expect
further attestation kinds and per-ticket CI-gate consumption later.

## What
Replace the slot with `state["attestations"]`, a map keyed by attestation kind (per-key
LWW, mirroring `process_workflow_run`): additive across kinds, latest within a kind. Kind
is derived from the already-signed `manifest[0]` (`plan-review` / `completion-verifier`),
so no `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump and existing signatures still verify. Reuse the `SIGNATURE`
event (add an unsigned `data["kind"]` for routing). Verification is per-kind. Attestations
are immutable signed records; validity for a gate is computed on read via a single
`compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind)` dispatcher.

## Migration (expand only; contract deferred)
- New code writes ONLY `state["attestations"]` in compiled state and writes ONLY plain
  `SIGNATURE` events (with `data["kind"]`); it never writes `state["signature"]` into events.
- Read fold-in: a ticket/snapshot carrying only the legacy `signature` is folded into
  `attestations` on read (kind from its manifest).
- SNAPSHOT mirror: a NEW snapshot ALSO synthesizes a legacy `state["signature"]` (= the
  most-recent attestation) so a rolled-back/not-yet-upgraded clone can still read one
  attestation. THIS EPIC KEEPS THE MIRROR PERMANENTLY (cheap; rollback-safe at all times).
- The "contract" phase (dropping the mirror) is OUT OF SCOPE here and tracked by a separate
  follow-up ticket gated on an explicit all-clones-upgraded readiness check + rollback path.

## Verification surface (explicit call-site migration)
- `verify_signature()` is migrated to read `state["attestations"][kind]` (with legacy
  `state["signature"]` fold-in), NOT the single slot. Without this, once new code stops
  writing `signature`, every caller would read `unsigned` for new tickets.
- The callers chaining through it are migrated to pass an explicit kind:
  `claim_gate_check` (attest.py), `drift_refresh_candidate` (attest.py),
  `refresh_attestation` (attest.py), `_child_closure_findings` (completion.py),
  CLI/MCP `verify_signature`.

## Validity model (computed on read; records never mutated)
- `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind)` centralizes per-kind rules; the
  MATERIAL-CHANGE field set (description/AC/file_impact/children) is a named constant.
- DATA SOURCE for "not reopened since": the reducer records `state["last_reopened_at"]` =
  timestamp of the most recent `closed -> open` transition (`process_status`/reopen path
  today writes only `state["status"]`). `compute_validity` checks
  `signed_at >= last_reopened_at` (or `last_reopened_at` is null) entirely from compiled
  state (no raw-event scan).
- completion-verifier valid iff: certified AND status==closed AND not-reopened-since AND
  material-fingerprint-unchanged-since `signed_at`.
- plan-review valid iff: existing freshness (material + code drift) AND not-reopened-since.
- MATERIAL CHANGE = a post-`signed_at` mutation to a field in the material set; tag/
  assignee/comment/link changes are NOT material. The completion signer records the material
  fingerprint in its manifest so the check is symmetric with plan-review.
- `retire_attested_pin` (the blank-`SIGNATURE`-on-reopen mutation, signing.py) is REMOVED;
  reopen invalidation is validity-on-read for both kinds.

## Success Criteria
- A ticket simultaneously holds plan-review AND completion attestations; signing one never
  clobbers the other; re-running a kind replaces only that kind.
- Compaction/SNAPSHOT round-trips all kinds; legacy single-signature tickets and old-clone
  snapshots still read/verify; a new snapshot is readable by old code via the mirror.
- A reopened ticket stops reading as completion-valid AND plan-review-valid with no record
  mutation; re-closing/re-reviewing re-signs the relevant kind.

## Validation
- Manual dogfood smoke-test: create a ticket, sign a plan-review PASS, sign a
  completion-verifier PASS, run `rebar show` and confirm BOTH kinds appear with neither
  overwriting the other; reopen and confirm both read not-valid; verify each kind via
  `rebar verify-signature --kind K`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `state["attestations"]` (kind-keyed, per-key LWW) replaces the single slot; `process_signature` routes by `data["kind"]` (fallback: `manifest[0]`).
- [ ] Signed canonical payload unchanged; every pre-existing signature still certifies.
- [ ] New code never writes `state["signature"]` into events; read fold-in covers legacy tickets/old-clone snapshots; new SNAPSHOTs synthesize the legacy mirror (kept permanently in this epic; contract deferred to the follow-up ticket).
- [ ] `verify_signature()` reads `attestations[kind]` (legacy fold-in); the enumerated callers (`claim_gate_check`, `drift_refresh_candidate`, `refresh_attestation`, `_child_closure_findings`, CLI/MCP) pass an explicit kind.
- [ ] Reducer records `state["last_reopened_at"]` (most recent closed->open); `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind)` uses it; MATERIAL-CHANGE set is a named constant.
- [ ] completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since; completion signer records the material fingerprint.
- [ ] plan-review valid = existing freshness AND not-reopened-since; `retire_attested_pin` removed; no transition mutates any attestation record.
- [ ] `verify_signature(ticket_id, kind=...)` certifies one kind; no kind returns `{kind: verdict}`; claim gate reads `plan-review`, close + child-closure read `completion-verifier`.
- [ ] `show`/MCP render the `attestations` map (omitted when empty).
- [ ] Tests: coexistence; within-kind replace; compaction round-trip; legacy + new-snapshot cross-read; reopen invalidates completion + plan-review validity without mutation; material-change invalidates completion; concurrent different-kind signs both survive.
## Design clarifications (review-driven)
- KIND AUTHORITY: the SIGNED `manifest[0]` is the authoritative source of an attestation's
  kind; `data["kind"]` is only a routing hint and MUST equal the `manifest[0]`-derived kind
  or it is ignored (never trust the unsigned envelope over the signed manifest).
- KIND PARSE PROTOCOL: kind = the substring of `manifest[0]` before the first ": " (e.g.
  "plan-review: PASS" -> "plan-review"); the kind vocabulary is a named enum/constant shared
  by the reducer, verify, and `compute_validity`.
- ATTESTATION-RECORD SCHEMA OWNER: 114f owns the canonical per-record shape
  (manifest, algorithm, signature, key_id, head_sha, signed_at, material_fingerprint?);
  2c2d/cc1b/ed26 reference it (no divergent definitions).
- compute_validity IS NOT pure-compiled-state: plan-review validity still performs the
  existing filesystem code-drift re-hash; only the reopen + material checks are pure
  compiled-state. "Computed on read" means "not stored/mutated", not "no I/O".
- MATERIAL set reuse: reuse the EXISTING plan-review material fingerprint definition as the
  single source of the material field set (no new, separately-owned constant to drift).
- last_reopened_at: `process_status` detects the closed->open transition from
  `data["current_status"]` (== "closed") + new status (== "open"); records the event timestamp.
- _present.py LEAK: `_INTERNAL_SUBKEYS` strips the HMAC hex at the OLD single-level
  `signature.signature` path; after migration it must strip at the TWO-level
  `attestations.<kind>.signature` path so the hex never leaks in show/MCP.
- ROLLBACK (this epic): safe at any time — events keep the unchanged `SIGNATURE` shape (old
  code replays them fine) and new SNAPSHOTs carry the legacy mirror, so reverting the code
  loses no attestation visibility.
